name: PowerShell Profile Modification
notes:
This rule triggers when a powershell profile is created or modified which could indicate suspicious activity as the profile can be used as a mean of persistence.

Covered from SIGMA Rule.

Original Author: HieuTT35, Nasreddine Bencherchali
SIGMA Title: PowerShell Profile Modification
SIGMA Description: Detects the creation or modification of a powershell profile which could indicate suspicious activity as the profile can be used as a mean of persistence


Confidence: LOW

This rule may be covered under the Detection Rule License: https://ibm.biz/SigmaDetectionRuleLicense
conditions_text: when an event matches any of the following BB:DeviceDefinition: Operating System
when the event category for the event is one of the following System.File Created, System.Successful File Modification
when the event matches Filename (custom) is not N/A
when the event matches Filename (custom) is any of [Microsoft.Powershell_profile.ps1 or profile.ps1 or Profile.ps1]

when the event matches File Directory (custom) is any of [C:\Program Files\PowerShell\7 or C:\Windows\System32\WindowsPowerShell\v1.0 or C:\PowerShell or C:\WindowsPowerShell or C:\Documents\PowerShell or C:\Documents\Microsoft\Powershell\7]
TD_list: APPLY PowerShell Profile Modification on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when an event matches any of the following BB:DeviceDefinition: Operating System
AND when the event category for the event is one of the following System.File Created, System.Successful File Modification
AND when the event matches Filename (custom) is not N/A
AND when the event matches Filename (custom) is any of [Microsoft.Powershell_profile.ps1 or profile.ps1 or Profile.ps1]

AND when the event matches File Directory (custom) is any of [C:\Program Files\PowerShell\7 or C:\Windows\System32\WindowsPowerShell\v1.0 or C:\PowerShell or C:\WindowsPowerShell or C:\Documents\PowerShell or C:\Documents\Microsoft\Powershell\7]
RED_list: Event description: Detects when a powershell profile is created or modified which could indicate suspicious activity as the profile can be used as a mean of persistence.
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Filename (custom)
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
